structure.description: Per-tab tri-state toggle (source / live-preview / reading) sits in tab header, not global preferences. Each open tab carries its own mode — user can have one note in source, another in reading mode side-by-side. Mode is materialization-of-same-content, not a workflow status.

rationale.hypothesis: When the same entity admits multiple presentational projections of the same content (raw / rendered / preview), per-instance mode beats global mode because users compare modes simultaneously. This is distinct from phase-aware-primary-cta (which is about workflow status driving CTA) — here mode is purely view, no state mutation.

evidence.description: Each tab has source/live-preview/reading toggle in tab header
Per-editor preview/source toggle for markdown — same pattern

counterexample.description: Single global edit/suggest/view mode — works because there is one document, not many tabs

shouldMatch.reason: Page admits source/rendered/reading-mode equivalents per tab

shouldNotMatch.reason: Order.status is a workflow status, not a view mode — phase-aware-primary-cta covers it
